title: Publish readiness blockers are revealed one at a time, in internal naming, with no full checklist
detail: Chain observed: 'Fix SEO title' → 'Add a hero headline before publishing.' → 'Fix content 1 · Cta Label' → 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL'. Each fix reveals the next; total work is never visible upfront.

suggested_fix.current: Single rotating blocker chip ('Fix content 1 · Cta Label')
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a persistent checklist of ALL remaining publish blockers in plain language, each deep-linking to (and opening) its field

title: Scheduling a publish gives zero confirmation — the field re-renders empty and no 'Scheduled for …' status exists anywhere
detail: After filling the datetime and saving, only a generic 'Draft saved.' toast fires; the field shows 'dd/mm/yyyy, --:-- --'. The only hint a schedule exists is the 'Cancel scheduled publish' checkbox. Flagged by all 15 personas.

suggested_fix.current: Field clears after save; no status indicator
suggested_fix.proposed: After save, keep the value in the field and show a persistent 'Scheduled to publish {date} {time} ({tz})' status in the Publish Status card with a cancel action

title: A publish-required field (CTA destination URL) exists only inside the Block actions → Edit settings modal, and the blocker text never says where it is
detail: 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' points at a field that is not on the canvas; users hunt and dead-end.

suggested_fix.current: Blocker names a field hidden two menu levels deep
suggested_fix.proposed: Make the blocker a link that opens the block settings modal at the field, and/or surface CTA destination inline on the canvas next to the CTA label

title: After publishing succeeds, the 'Confirm publish' card stays open re-asking to publish
detail: 'Changes published.' is announced twice (pill + banner) while the yellow confirm card still shows active Cancel/Confirm publish buttons reading 'This will replace the current live version…'. Success is ambiguous and invites a double publish.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm card persists after successful publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Dismiss the confirm card on success and replace it with a success state containing an 'Open live page' link

title: Redirects cannot be edited or deleted — a mistyped 301 is permanent from the admin UI
detail: Table has five columns (Old path/Destination/Status/Source/Created) and no actions. 301s are cached aggressively by browsers and search engines, so mistakes have lasting SEO impact.

suggested_fix.current: No row actions in redirects table
suggested_fix.proposed: Add per-row Edit and Delete (with confirm) actions; consider a 'disable' state for safe rollback

title: Leaving the editor with unsaved typed content gives no warning — and silently creates an orphan draft
detail: Verified mechanism (corrected in verification): typing a title and navigating away shows no unsaved-changes prompt; the content is silently persisted as a new draft page the user never asked to create ('Unsaved data-loss probe' appeared as a draft with its own /resources/ slug). User cannot tell whether work was lost or saved.

suggested_fix.current: Silent navigation away; orphan draft created without consent
suggested_fix.proposed: Intercept navigation with an unsaved-changes prompt (Save draft / Discard / Keep editing), and never persist a draft without an explicit save

title: Publish-gate messaging contradicts itself: chip vs readiness panel vs the field's own placeholder
detail: One frame shows chip 'Fix SEO title' + pill 'Blocked' while the SEO title field says 'Leave blank to use page headline' (the real gate was the empty hero headline). Chip wording also drifts ('Fix' vs 'Improve SEO title').

suggested_fix.current: Three surfaces with different messages for the same gate
suggested_fix.proposed: Drive all blocker surfaces from one source of truth, naming the actual missing field ('Add a hero headline')

title: Autosave fails silently — 'seo page autosave failed TypeError: Failed to fetch' appears only in the console
detail: Editor showed no UI error while autosave failed; user believes work is saved. Severity from impact (silent data-loss class), not frequency.

suggested_fix.current: Autosave failure logged to console only
suggested_fix.proposed: Surface a visible 'Autosave failed — retrying / Save manually' banner and retry with backoff

title: Disabled Publish button exposes no programmatic reason; blocker is small orange text/title attr only
detail: Screen-reader users cannot tell what is gating publish.

suggested_fix.current: Blocker reason in adjacent text and title attribute
suggested_fix.proposed: Use aria-describedby from the Publish button to the blocker list and announce blocker changes via a live region

title: WITHDRAWN — 'Pacific Time for an Australian business' was a wrong premise: Vendingpreneurs is a US West Coast business, so Pacific Time is correct
detail: Originally flagged as P1 by 11 personas based on the reviewer's incorrect assumption that the business is Australian. Owner confirmed US West Coast on 2026-06-10. The schedule field's explicit timezone label is good practice. The silent-save problem (P0-2) is unaffected.

suggested_fix.current: n/a
suggested_fix.proposed: n/a — no change needed for timezone

title: Readiness and Status columns are colour-only dots with no visible text or legend
detail: Verification correction: dots ARE buttons with accessible names (tooltips on hover/focus), so this is a visual/glanceability issue, not an AT blocker.

suggested_fix.current: Bare coloured dot per row
suggested_fix.proposed: Dot + visible text label (e.g. 'Needs work', 'Published') or a column legend

title: aria-prohibited-attr (serious): aria-label on spans for Hero/FAQ block markers is dropped by AT

suggested_fix.current: aria-label on role-less spans
suggested_fix.proposed: Move the accessible name onto a focusable control or give the wrapper an appropriate role

title: No revisions until first publish — a draft mangled before publishing has no page-level undo

suggested_fix.current: Revisions created only on publish/refresh/restore
suggested_fix.proposed: Snapshot a revision on every manual 'Save draft' (or periodically) so drafting has restore points

title: No 'Open live page' link surfaces after publishing; public URL 404'd briefly right after publish
detail: A Share → 'Copy public URL' affordance exists but is hidden in a dropdown; the URL had to be typed manually and returned 404 on immediate polls.

suggested_fix.current: Live link only via Share dropdown
suggested_fix.proposed: Show 'Open live page' in the publish success state and Publish Status card; debounce until route is live

title: Editor on a 375px phone is one marathon scroll: Save/Publish in different scroll regions from content; no mobile layout

suggested_fix.current: Desktop panels stacked vertically on mobile
suggested_fix.proposed: Sticky mobile action bar (Save/Publish/status) and collapsible SEO panel

title: Redirect form error 'Path must be root-relative.' is jargon, renders as a top banner, and clears the entered values

suggested_fix.current: 'Path must be root-relative.' + cleared fields
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Start the path with / (for example /resources/old-page)' shown next to the field, preserving entered values

title: New-page canvas is all grey placeholder text with no cue which fields are required to publish

suggested_fix.current: Ghost text 'Hero headline' etc. with no affordance
suggested_fix.proposed: Mark publish-required fields ('Required to publish') and add an empty-state checklist

title: SEO panel is an information dump (readiness, 5 fields, 8 score rows, 7 action items, governance, comments) with no hierarchy

suggested_fix.current: Seven stacked sections in one scroll
suggested_fix.proposed: Collapse to readiness verdict + 'what to fix next'; move governance/comments behind tabs

title: 3-step wizard (incl. a review-only step 3) stands between 'Create page' and typing anything

suggested_fix.current: Type → starting point → review → editor
suggested_fix.proposed: One step (type + starting point combined), straight into the editor

title: No keyboard shortcuts (save/publish/insert block)

suggested_fix.current: None
suggested_fix.proposed: Cmd+S save, Cmd+Enter publish, '/' block insert

title: Forced 'Quick Tour · Step 1 of 3' overlay covers the canvas on first editor open

suggested_fix.current: Auto-opening tour
suggested_fix.proposed: Dismissible 'Take a tour?' affordance, never auto-blocking

title: Unexplained jargon across the builder: 'Eyebrow', 'Slug', 'CTA', 'Governance comments', 'Builder support'

suggested_fix.current: 'Eyebrow'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Overline (small label above the heading)' + first-use expansion of CTA; 'Slug' → 'URL ending'

title: Tab order buries the form: SEO fields are stops 28–33, behind the whole rendered page preview

suggested_fix.current: Canvas preview consumes early tab stops
suggested_fix.proposed: Skip-links to panel/canvas; make preview chrome non-tabbable

title: Landmark violations: nested complementary sidebar, duplicate <main> on revision preview, duplicate named regions in wizard

suggested_fix.current: Nested/duplicated landmarks
suggested_fix.proposed: Single top-level main per page; sidebar complementary at document level

title: A nextjs-portal element receives keyboard focus with no visible focus indicator on every page

suggested_fix.current: Focusable portal wrapper
suggested_fix.proposed: Remove portal wrapper from tab order or give it a visible focus style

title: Floating 'N' avatar button overlaps/clips the Sign out button in the sidebar on every page

suggested_fix.current: Overlapping FAB
suggested_fix.proposed: Reposition FAB clear of the sidebar footer

title: Duplicate page generates a junk slug ('draft-4020bd0f') instead of deriving from the source

suggested_fix.current: Random hash slug
suggested_fix.proposed: '{source-slug}-copy' with uniqueness suffix

title: Revision preview is sparse and confusing: lone lowercase 'publish' label, AM/PM time mismatch vs editor, minimal context

suggested_fix.current: 'Revision preview / publish - Jun 10, 3:49 PM'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Version {n} — saved when the page was published, {local datetime}' with consistent timezone
